contract: {rating, notes} — exactly two keys, rating int 0-100, notes non-empty, one bare JSON object
comment: schema_cases run with NO model (pure Python via memresource_spec.schema_ok) and MUST pass exactly as marked — the deterministic forcing function for output shape. band_cases are the labeled held-out examples; the runner checks the reference oracle lands in band (a known-good baseline) and that an empty emission scores 0.0 (cannot saturate). A live agent's recorded outputs can be checked the same way once a run exists. Prose is never compared.

output.notes: Every resource is released on all paths and nothing grows without bound; no change needed.

Unbounded growth: _cache[k] is written on every call and never evicted, so the dict grows without limit and exhausts memory over time; bound it with functools.lru_cache(maxsize=N) or an LRU/TTL eviction policy to reach 100.
use-after-close: f.read() runs after f.close(), operating on a released handle; move the read before close() or use a with-block so the file is read while open to fix

Retained reference: each handler is appended to a module-level list that is never cleared, preventing collection of the closures and their captured state; drop the reference (or use a WeakSet / explicit removal) when the handler is done to reach 100

band_cases.input_code:
with open(path) as f:
    data = f.read()
